findings.line: "I was never going to be enough alone," she said. "Not at the border. Not in Lordaeron. Not in the throne room."
findings.deletionTest: "I was never going to be enough alone," she said.
findings.reason: KILL — pattern 7 chained fragment negation; the place list still works once the line commits directly instead of stair-stepping through three refusals.
findings.suggestedFix: "I was never going to be enough alone at the border, in Lordaeron, or in the throne room," she said.

findings.line: "We stopped him because we were right together. Not because I was right and you were useful."
findings.deletionTest: "We stopped him because we were right together."
findings.reason: KILL — pattern 3 source-swap pivot; the second sentence only rejects a false frame the first sentence has already replaced.
findings.suggestedFix: "We stopped him because we were right together, with both of us doing the work."

findings.line:
But the thing in Sylvanas's chest was not Frostmourne.

Kalar'shorel'anor.
findings.deletionTest: But the thing in Sylvanas's chest was Kalar'shorel'anor.
findings.reason: KILL — pattern 2 fragment reveal; the negated setup is scaffolding for the named answer on the next line.
findings.suggestedFix: But the thing in Sylvanas's chest was Kalar'shorel'anor.

audits.dominantPattern: body-part agency and abstract nouns carrying causal weight
clusterFlags: Late visitor exchange carries repeated abstract/body-part agency constructions
Smart-POV sections show multiple agency-laundering hits

findings.line: The next step always close enough to excuse the one after it.
findings.subject: next step
findings.realAgent: Arthas and Kel'Thuzad kept using the next move to justify the move before it.
findings.reason: KILL — the step is not the actor doing the excusing; the men are the ones rationalizing their sequence.
findings.suggestedFix: They kept using the next step to justify the one before it.

findings.line: The eyes traveled over her face with scholarly patience.
findings.subject: eyes
findings.realAgent: The creature studied her face with scholarly patience.
findings.reason: KILL — pattern 8 body-part agency; the creature, not its eyes, performs the inspection.
findings.suggestedFix: It studied her face with scholarly patience.

findings.line: Its gaze lifted slightly, toward the vault above.
findings.subject: gaze
findings.realAgent: The creature angled its head toward the vault above.
findings.reason: KILL — pattern 8 body-part agency; the creature is the one redirecting attention, not the gaze acting by itself.
findings.suggestedFix: Its head angled slightly toward the vault above.

findings.subject: words
findings.realAgent: Neither of them spoke while the line landed between them.
findings.reason: KILL — the sentence gives inert words physical occupancy instead of naming the silence and human pause that do the work.
findings.suggestedFix: Neither of them spoke for a beat.

findings.line: Jaina had said the same thing through the wall, and hearing it here—hearing it from the mouth of a thing that spoke of Frostmourne as its craft—gave the truth a deeper chill.
findings.subject: truth
findings.realAgent: Hearing the same claim from Frostmourne's maker chilled Jaina more deeply.
findings.reason: KILL — pattern 5 object/abstraction agency; the truth is not the active giver of the chill.
findings.suggestedFix: Jaina had said the same thing through the wall, and hearing it here—from the mouth of a thing that spoke of Frostmourne as its craft—chilled her more deeply.

findings.subject: Recognition
findings.realAgent: The creature showed recognition for a beat.
findings.reason: KILL — pattern 9 nominalized verb as agent; the recognition does not act independently of the creature showing it.
findings.suggestedFix: For a beat, it showed recognition.

findings.subject: eyes
findings.realAgent: The creature kept its attention on her.
findings.reason: KILL — pattern 8 body-part agency; the creature is maintaining the lock, not the eyes acting alone.
findings.suggestedFix: It kept its attention on her.

findings.line: The better question came at once.
findings.subject: question
findings.realAgent: Sylvanas thought of the better question at once.
findings.reason: KILL — pattern 9 nominalized thought acting as subject; Sylvanas is the one making the mental move.
findings.suggestedFix: Sylvanas thought of the better question at once.

findings.line: That meant it sat deeper than discipline.
findings.subject: it
findings.realAgent: The farewell belonged to a habit older than discipline.
findings.reason: KILL — the phrase is not physically sitting anywhere; the sentence is borrowing bodily placement to avoid naming what the clue implies.
findings.suggestedFix: That meant the habit behind it belonged to something older than discipline.

findings.line: A network could listen for words.
findings.subject: network
findings.realAgent: Farstriders, quartermasters, runners, and archive mages could listen for the phrase.
findings.reason: KILL — the sentence hands a human verb to an abstraction when the very next line can name the people who would do the listening.
findings.suggestedFix: Farstriders, quartermasters, runners, and archive mages could listen for words.

findings.line: The eyes traveled over her face with scholarly patience.
findings.reason: KILL — the chapter runs past the four-beat eye/gaze cap; this is one of the weaker generic ocular beats and it doubles a body-part agency construction.
findings.suggestedFix: It studied her face with scholarly patience.

findings.line: Its gaze lifted slightly, toward the vault above.
findings.reason: KILL — the chapter already has stronger eye-based beats; this one is generic enough to convert cleanly to head movement.
findings.suggestedFix: Its head angled slightly toward the vault above.

summary.verdictReason: Pass A still has more than three MILD KILL findings after revision, driven by ten agency-laundering lines, three negation pivots, and two excess eye/gaze beats.
